title: URM Weaker Pier Bed Joint Sliding Classification Guide

identification_guidelines.by_observation: Sliding occurs on bed joints in two basic forms: sliding on a horizontal plane, and a stair-stepped diagonal crack where head joints open and close to allow for movement on the bed joint. Pure bed joint sliding is a ductile mode with significant hysteretic energy absorption capability. Gradual degradation occurs until instability is reached.
identification_guidelines.by_analysis: Stair-stepped cracking may resemble diagonal tension cracking; confirm by analysis that bed joint sliding governs.
identification_guidelines.note: Figures show a single crack for simplicity, but multiple cracks stepping in each direction are possible under cyclic loading.

criteria: Hairline cracks/spalled mortar in head and bed joints either on a horizontal plane or in a stair-stepped fashion.
No offset along the crack has occurred and the crack/stair-stepping is not continuous across the pier.
damage_levels.typical_appearance_desc: Diagram shows a single stair-stepped crack starting from the side and terminating before reaching the other side of the pier (1).
restoration_measures: Not necessary for restoration of structural performance.
Measures may be necessary for restoration of nonstructural characteristics.

criteria: Horizontal cracks/spalled mortar at bed joints indicating in-plane offset occurred and/or opening of head joints up to ~1/4", creating a stair-stepped pattern.
5% of courses or fewer have cracks in masonry units.
damage_levels.typical_appearance_desc: Diagram (1) shows a continuous stair-stepped crack spanning the width of the pier with (2) indicating minor masonry unit cracking.

restoration_measures.full_restoration: Replacement or enhancement required for full restoration of seismic performance.
partial_restoration: Repoint spalled mortar and open head joints
Inject cracks and open head joints
restoration_measures.caution: Grout injection may increase strength but decrease deformation capacity by changing behavior to a less ductile mode.

criteria: Horizontal cracks/spalled mortar at bed joints indicating in-plane offset occurred and/or opening of head joints up to ~1/2", creating a stair-stepped pattern.
5% of courses or fewer have cracks in masonry units.
damage_levels.typical_appearance_desc: Diagram indicates more pronounced stair-stepped cracking (1) and masonry unit cracking (2) than the Moderate level.

restoration_measures.full_restoration: Replacement or enhancement required for full restoration of seismic performance.
partial_restoration: Repoint spalled mortar and open head joints
Inject cracks and open head joints

criteria: Stair-stepped movement is so significant that upper bricks have slid off their supporting brick.
Cracks have propagated into a significant number of courses of units.
Residual set is so significant that portions of masonry at edges of the pier have begun or are about to fall.
